message:
That cozy rumble your cat makes when they curl up in your lap? 🐱💤 It might be doing more good than you ever realized — for both of you.

A cat's purr usually vibrates somewhere between 25 and 150 Hertz, and that's where things get fascinating. Scientists have found that these same gentle, low frequencies are the ones used in therapy to help heal bones, soothe aching muscles, and ease pain and swelling. In other words, your cat is humming along in the exact range that helps bodies repair themselves.

This may be why cats often purr not only when they're blissfully happy, but also when they're hurt, frightened, or recovering — many researchers think it's a built-in self-soothing and self-healing system. And when they purr against you? That soft vibration can lower stress, calm your heart rate, and melt away the tension of a long day. No wonder a purring cat on your chest feels like instant therapy. 🥰

Does your cat have a favorite spot to settle in and purr? Comment 🐾 and tell me where your little healer likes to curl up!
